# AppDataRoot

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostinvariantsdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host invariants](../invariants.md)

`internal/hostintegration.AppDataRoot` is the pure C11 model for approved app data roots. The model does not call OS APIs; it validates the channel, host OS, and root candidates supplied by adapters.

```text
AppDataRoot {
    channel DistributionChannel
    hostOS  "darwin" | "windows"
    scope   "user-application-support" | "sandbox-container" | "app-group" |
            "windows-local-app-data" | "windows-package-local-data"
    path    string
}
```

1. `dev-local` and `developer-id` on macOS use `$HOME/Library/Application Support/riido`.
2. `mac-app-store` requires an app group root or sandbox container root supplied by an adapter; user home fallback is rejected.
3. `msix-sideload` and `msix-store` require Windows package local data roots supplied by an adapter; user profile fallback is rejected.
4. C6 materializes workdirs only under `AppDataRoot.WorkdirRoot()`, which appends `workspaces/` under the approved app data root.
5. App data root is not the user's repository root; user-selected workspace folders are represented by `WorkspaceGrantStore`.
